properties.remark: SPOTTER REPORTED A PINE TREE WAS UPROOTED AND FELL ON A HOUSE ON MEISENHEIMER ROAD SOUTH OF LUDINGTON. ALSO REPORTED STANDING WATER ON THE ROAD AT SCHOENER AND DEWEY ROADS.
ROOFS OFF MULTIPLE STRUCTURES WITH CAMPERS BLOWN OVER
A TRAILER AND LOG CABIN HAVE LOST THEIR ROOF WHILE A BARN SUFFERED HEAVY DAMAGE. POWER LINES AND SEVERAL TREES HAVE ALSO BEEN REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA.
HAIL IS COVERING THE GROUND.

NUMEROUS TREE GREAT THAN 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER REPORTED DOWN. SPOTTER ALSO REPORTS THAT NUMEROUS BUSINESSES HAVE SUFFERED WINDOW DAMAGE IN MUSKEGON. ALL TREES WERE LAYING IN A SOUTH-SOUTHEAST DIRECTION.
SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND ESTIMATED WINDS OF 55 MILES PER HOUR
SPOTTER REPORTED HAIL COVERING THE GROUND. MOST OF IT WAS QUARTER SIZE.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL CAME DOWN HEAVILY AND COVERED THE ROADWAY
FIRE-AMBULANCE RESPONSE TEAM CALLED TO REPORT ESTIMATED WINDS OF 65 MPH. STATED THAT SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF A ROOF ON A BUILDING NEAR THE FACILITY.
HAM RADIO OPERATOR CALLED IN TO REPORT WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 60 MPH. A TREE WAS SNAPPED IN HALF NEAR MACARTHUR ROAD THAT MEASURES ABOUT 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER
PINES TREES BLOWN OVER AND BARN ROOF DAMAGED
COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS A PLANE FLIPPED OVER AT THE GRANT AIRPORT. ALSO A METAL HANGAR WAS DESTROYED WITH DEBRIS SCATTERED ALL AROUND.
LOCAL FORD DEALER HAS SUFFERED SEVERE ROOF DAMAGE
LOCAL AND STATE POLICE HAVE ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO BE AT LEAST 80 MPH.
KENT COUNTY SHERRIF STATED THAT A GARAGE SUFFERED DAMAGE ALONG CENTENNIAL STREET
SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED NUMEROUS 2 TO 4 INCH TREE LIMBS DOWN. POWER IS OUT IN THE AREA AS WELL.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL COVERED THE GROUND. NUMEROUS TREES ALSO REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA.
REPORT OF SMALL TREE LIMBS DOWN. SHINGLES HAVE BLOWN OFF NEARBY HOUSES AS WELL HAS MAILBOX AND FENCE BLOWN OVER.
ROOF PANELING HAS BEEN TORN OFF THE FIRE STATION. POWER LINES ARE DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES ARE BEING REPORTED. PEA SIZE HAIL ACCOMPANIED THE STORM.
REPORTS OF MULTIPLE TYPES OF DAMAGE IN SUMMIT AND RIVERTON TOWNSHIPS. DAMAGE INCLUDES IRRIGATION PIVOTS TIPPED OVER...TREES DOWN...AND DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS.
SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE IN GREEN TOWNSHIP. TIME APPROXIMATE. TWO OVERTURNED TRAILERS AND SEVERAL LARGE TREES UPROOTED.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS THAT A TRAILER HAS BEEN BLOWN OVER AND NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES IN BIG RAPIDS.
REPORTS OF A BARN DOOR PUSHED IN...A YARD SHED DESTROYED...AND SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A GARAGE ROOF AT A RESIDENCE.
POSSIBLE BARN ROOF BLOWN OFF.

NWS EMPLOYEE RELAYED INFORMATION FROM RELATIVES/FRIENDS THAT A BARN HAD BEEN COMPLETELY DESTROYED...SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND A PONTOON BOAT WAS LOFTED INTO THE MIDDLE OF AN OPEN FIELD. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A FEW HEALTHY TREES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF JERSEYVILLE ROAD AND WYMAN ROAD. ONE TREE IS ON A HOUSE.

WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE IN SOUTHWEST ISABELLA COUNTY. MANY UPROOTED/SNAPPED TREES...INCLUDING A LARGE SUGAR MAPLE THAT FELL ON A HOUSE ON WAYMAN ROAD. ALSO IN THE AREA OF WAYMAN AND JERSEYVILLE ROADS...A SHED WAS DESTROYED AND A CAMPER WAS FLIPPED AT LEAST ONCE AND IS NOW UPSIDE DOWN UP AGAINST THE HOUSE. A COUPLE IRRIGATION PIVOTS HAVE ALSO BEEN FLIPPED IN FIELDS.
DAMAGE TO A GARAGE ROOF AND SOME POWER LINES DOWN.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN WITH SOME VEHICLES DAMAGED. PART OF A ROOF IS IN THE ROAD. RELAYED FROM WWTV.

ON GUN LAKE...BOATS WERE LOOSENED WITH SOME TORN CANOPIES. A FEW STORAGE SHEDS CAME APART AND SOME CAR PORTS WERE DAMAGED.

MICHIGAN STATE POLICE CALLED IN SOME SMALL TREES THAT WERE DOWN ALONG GUN LAKE ROAD. EXACT SIZE IS UNKNOWN AT THE TIME BUT TREES WERE SMALL ENOUGH TO BE PHYSICALLY REMOVED FROM THE ROADWAY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

BARRY CO. CENTRAL DISPATCH STATED THAT THEY RECEIVED ADDITIONAL REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ALONG GUN LAKE ROAD. POWER OUTAGES ARE ALSO ONGOING WEST OF HASTINGS...NORTH OF THE M-43 AND M-37 INTERSECTION AND HASTINGS AIRPORT. TIME OF INCIDENT WAS REFERENCED BY CONSUMERS ENERGY OUTAGE REPORTS.

SPOKE TO KALAMAZOO DISPATCH. THEY ARE RECEIVING SEVERAL REPORTS OF TREE AND POWER LINES DOWN AT THE TIME. A HOUSE FIRE WAS CALLED IN POSSIBLY DUE TO WIRES/BLOWN TRANSFORMER.
SPOTTER REPORTED POWER SURGES. NEIGHBORS LOST POWER AND ARE CURRENTLY WITHOUT POWER.

SPOTTER IS REPORTING EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE THROUGHOUT THE AREA. MANY ROADS HAVE BEEN BLOCKED DUE TO DOWNED TREES AROUND VICKSBURG AND PORTAGE AREAS. WINDS STARTED AROUND 120 AM AND DID NOT END UNTIL AROUND 140 AM
SPOTTER WITNESSED SEVERAL TREES COME DOWN ALONG WITH POWER FLASHES IN THE AREA.

TREE DOWN AT PORT SHELDON ST. AND US-31. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

*** 6 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN BYRON CENTER BEFORE MOVING INTO WYOMING AND THEN KENTWOOD BEFORE LIFTING. THE TORNADO HAD MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 6.25 MILES AND THE WIDTH WAS 300-400 YARDS.
2 BUILDINGS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. AT LEAST 10 TREES UPROOTED AND ON BUILDINGS AND BLOCKING THE ROAD. NO POWER IN THE AREA.
5 OR 6 RVS FLIPPED OVER AT RV DEALER LOCATED AT 5600 DIVISION AVE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
HEAVY DAMAGE TO INDUSTRIAL BUILDING JUST SOUTH OF 56TH ST AND DIVISION AVE. TIME OF DAMAGE IS RADAR ESTIMATED. REPORT COURTESY OF WOODTV.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN, ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF HOUSES AND SOME PEOPLE TRAPPED. THE DAMAGE WAS IN A LINE FROM 54TH AND DIVISION EAST NORTHEAST TO 44TH STREET.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE REPORTED ALONG DIVISION AVE. BETWEEN 52ND AND 60TH STREETS IN KENTWOOD. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. ALSO REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES AND ROOFS WITH INSULATION IN THE ROAD.
PLYWOOD DRIVEN THROUGH BRICK SIDING OF HOME IN KENTWOOD. REPORT COURTESY OF WOODTV. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO HOME AND VEHICLES NEAR 52ND AND EASTERN AVE IN KENTWOOD. REPORT COURTESY OF WOODTV. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MARSHALL AVE IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
POWER LINES DOWN AND A TREE DOWN ON A HOUSE AT 48TH AND MILDRED IN KENTWOOD.
LARGE TREES AND BRANCHES HAVE BEEN SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. POWER LINES DOWN. EXTREME WINDS.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN IONIA COUNTY ALONG REEDER ROAD BETWEEN SOUTH SUNFIELD HIGHWAY AND SOUTH KEEFER HIGHWAY. FIVE FARM BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED AND ONE HOME LOST ROOFING MATERIAL. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DAMAGED OR KNOCKED OVER AND SOME CROP DAMAGE WAS ALSO OBSERVED. PATH LENGTH ABOUT 1 MILE. WIDTH ABOUT 50 YARDS. MAX WIND ESTIMATED 75 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH A DAMAGE PATH BEGINNING NEAR PINE ROAD JUST SOUTH OF KIMBALL ROAD. THE PATH CONTINUED NORTHEAST APPROXIMATELY 0.75 MILES AND CROSSED STONEY CREEK ROAD THEN ENDED JUST SOUTH OF M-21. TWO HOMES LOST ROOFING MATERIAL AND TWO FARM BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. A PLASTIC PLAYGROUND SLIDE WAS CARRIED ABOUT 1500 FEET. SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED IN A PATH ABOUT 50 YARDS WIDE WITH LIGHTER DAMAGE EXTENDING OUT TO ABOUT 150 YARDS WIDE. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH. TORNADO DURATION 4 MINUTES.

WINDS ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH DESTROYED A BARN AND AN ATTACHED GARAGE AND DAMAGED SIDING ON A HOUSE. THE DAMAGE WAS VERY ISOLATED AND RESTRICTED TO THIS ONE RESIDENCE ON TALLMAN ROAD JUST SOUTH OF WALKER ROAD. TSTM WND DMG DURATION 2 MINUTES.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH TOP WINDS ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH IN CLINTON COUNTY ON JULY 7TH AROUND 1 AM EDT. THE DAMAGE BEGAN JUST SOUTH OF WALKER ROAD BETWEEN ESSEX CENTER AND LOWELL ROADS. DAMAGE TO TREES AND OUTBUILDINGS OCCURRED ALONG WALKER ROAD. THE TORNADO MOVED THROUGH OPEN FIELDS ALONG LOWELL AND KINLEY ROADS BEFORE STRIKING A WOODED AREA NORTH OF KINLEY ROAD AND WEST OF GROVE ROAD WHERE THERE WAS A COMPLETE BLOWDOWN OF LARGE OAK AND MAPLE TREES IN TWO AREAS. THE LARGEST SWATH OF DOWNED TREES WAS ABOUT 150 YARDS LONG BY 80 YARDS WIDE. TORNADO DURATION 10 MINUTES.

AT LEAST 6-8 INCHES OF WATER OVER THE ROAD ON PINE AVENUE IN FRONT OF ALMA SENIOR HIGH SCHOOL. NUMEROUS STREETS IN ALMA AS WELL AS ITHACA WITH QUITE A BIT OF STANDING WATER. AT LEAST A FEW STREETS WILL BE BARRICADED.
WLNS REPORTS STANDING WATER AT HASLETT RD AND SAGINAW HWY.
SPOTTER REPORTS STANDING WATER IN YARD AND OVER ROAD NEAR HOUSE. BATTLE CREEK RIVER NEARBY IS RAPIDLY RISING.
